I therefore justify the structure from established sigmoidal-growth theory: the **Richards (generalized-logistic) curve** is the canonical single-parameter generalization that nests the logistic (shape=1) and approaches Gompertz as the shape parameter grows, and is widely used precisely because amyloid/polymerization curves show *variable* asymmetry between the post-lag rise and the plateau approach. A fitted shape exponent lets each dataset select its own asymmetry rather than being locked to one fixed form — exactly the limitation of the current Gompertz template.
